Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Business Records To Local Union 913 - Painters And Drywall Finishers
Off Calendar Jan-03-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFF JANE DOE's, AN INDIVIDUAL, Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Business Records To Local Union 913 - Painters And Drywall Finishers.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):

Melody Rissell Leonard, Esq., Rissell Law, APC, (415) 874-7288, for Plaintiff.

Kelly Aviva Cohen, Esq., of Seyfarth Shaw, LLP, (310) 277-7200, for Defendants.

The matter is argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling. Motion granted as to Categories 1-5, 8, 9, and 12-14. Counsel for defendant is reminded that courtesy copies of all filings must be provided to the Court and counsel must comply with Local Rule 2.7B. Requests 1 through 5, 8, 9, and 12-14 are overbroad and invade Plaintiff's privacy. The Court engaged in the privacy analysis set forth in Hill v. Nat'l Collegiate Athl. Asss'n. (1994) 7 Cal.4th 1, Valley Bank of Nevada v. Superior Court (1975) 15 Cal.3d 652 and their progeny and finds that the Plaintiff's right to privacy outweighs the Defendant's right to the discovery based upon the showing made as to the specific categories identified. Although Defendant may seek discovery relating to mitigation of damages and Plaintiff's employment at Coast to Coast, the specific items which are the subject of the motion are overbroad and constitute an invasion of privacy.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/MEW)

Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Business Records To District Council 16 International Union Of Painters And Allied Trades
Off Calendar Jan-03-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFF JANE DOE's, AN INDIVIDUAL, Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Business Records To District Council 16 International Union Of Painters And Allied Trades.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):

Melody Rissell Leonard, Esq., Rissell Law, APC, (415) 874-7288, for Plaintiff.

Kelly Aviva Cohen, Esq., of Seyfarth Shaw, LLP, (310) 277-7200, for Defendants.

The matter is argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling. Motion GRANTED as to Categories 1-5, 8, 9, and 12-14. No opposition filed. Even if the Court were to consider the Opposition filed in the companion Motion to Quash the Subpoena for Business Records directed to Local Union 913, for the reasons set forth in the Court's ruling on that motion, the ruling remains the same.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/MEW)
